channel.title: Posts on PapersCrawler Reports
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on PapersCrawler Reports

item.title: 文献报告
item.description:
文献报告

目录

  • 1. Analysis of velocity gradients inside dense heated titanium foils via space resolved H-like Lyman- α x-ray line formation
  • 2. Experimental demonstration of dephasing reduction in an optically guided laser-plasma accelerator
  • 3. Time-resolved interferometric measurements of plasma density evolution in laser-driven capacitor-coil targets
  • 4. Optimization of the characteristics of a relativistic electron beam based on laser wake-field acceleration using a non-symmetric gas target profile
  • 5. Spectral and spatial ion beam parameter optimization at petawatt laser facilities
  • 6. Online electron beam trajectory correction in free-electron lasers via adaptive dictionary learning
  • 7. Time-resolved characterisation of a 10 m discharge plasma for AWAKE
  • 8. CORRIGENDUM: Ultralow–emittance electron beams from laser wakefield accelerator based on sharp density transition injection (2026 Plasma Phys. Control. Fusion 68 045036)
  • 9. Broad-bandwidth x-ray free-electron lasers driven by plasma wakefield accelerators
  • 10. Highly efficient multi-cycle terahertz radiation driven by counter-propagating modulated wakefields in a homogeneous plasma
  • 11. Weibel-mediated filamentary structures observed in the ICF context
  • 12. Generation of trains of pulses with arbitrary delay and adjustable width from a petawatt-class laser
  • 13. An inverse-Cherenkov dielectric laser accelerator boosted by surface plasmon polaritons
  • 14. A transmission grating extreme ultraviolet spectrometer using a gradient-reflectivity Al/Zr multilayer mirror for the range of 24–32 nm
  • 15. The 2 PW/1 Hz laser system for laser-driven proton tumor therapy facility CLAPA- II : Compact laser plasma accelerator II
  • 16. Anomalous collisionally induced transparency
  • 17. Coherent radiation of ultrashort electron-bunches from linear acceleration
  • 18. Nanometer-scale prebunched electron beams generated from all-optical plasma-based acceleration
  • 19. Laser-driven autoresonant acceleration of thermal electrons in plasma solenoid
  • 20. Short overview of solid, gas, cryogenic and liquid target fabrication for single-beam high-power laser experiments
  • 21. Anomalous lepton acceleration in the radiation reaction dominated reflection regime
  • 22. Multiobjective Bayesian optimization for the shape design of rf cavity in particle accelerators
  • 23. Muon injection and acceleration via ultraintense electron beam-driven plasma wakefield
  • 24. Energy Bunching from Subcycle Ionization Injection in Laser Wakefield Acceleration
  • 25. Probing short-lived nuclear isomers in laser-induced plasmas
  • 26. Efficient plasma-based polarization converter for intense X-ray lasers
  • 27. Experimental Evidence of Vortex γ Photons in All-Optical Inverse Compton Scattering
  • 28. Identification of optimal conditions for betatron x-ray generation in subcritical density plasma
  • 29. Coincident learning for beam-based rf station fault identification using phase information at the SLAC linac coherent light source
  • 30. Dosimetric characterization of the laser-accelerated high-energy electron beam for radiotherapy applications
  • 31. Coherent Synchrotron Radiation by Excitation of Surface Plasmon Polariton on Near-Critical Solid Microtube Surface
  • 32. Strong-field ionization in particle-in-cell simulations
  • 33. Analysis of the Blowout Plasma Wakefields Produced by Drive Beams with Elliptical Symmetry
  • 34. N -dimensional maximum-entropy tomography via particle sampling
  • 35. Reduced Model of Ionization Lag in Intense Laser-Produced Plasmas
  • 36. Ion Acoustic Instability Resulting from Suprathermal Electrons Generated by Stimulated Raman Scattering in Laser-Plasma Interactions
  • 37. Accelerated three-dimensional quasistatic particle-in-cell code
  • 38. Self-calibration technique for characterization of integrated THz waveguides
  • 39. Thresholds for loss of Landau damping in longitudinal plane
  • 40. Guiding of high-intensity laser pulses in 100-mm-long hydrodynamic optical-field-ionized plasma channels
  • 41. Experimental study of wakefields driven by a self-modulating proton bunch in plasma

Analysis of velocity gradients inside dense heated titanium foils via space resolved H-like Lyman- α x-ray line formation

作者: F B Rosmej, O Renner, F P Condamine, An Tauschwitz, M Basko
日期: 2026-06-12
DOI: 10.1088/1361-6587/ae7428
页面: 链接
PDF: 下载
